pages.name: Home
pages.description: Landing page introducing the author, featured books, latest blog posts, and upcoming events. Acts as the primary hub for navigation to books, blog, events, library, and author info, and exposes a global book search bar that routes into the main Books & Search experience.
primary_functions: Give an overview of the author, featured book(s), and key site sections
Provide quick navigation to Books, Blog, Events, Library, About, and Contact pages
Offer a global search bar for books that leads to the Books & Search page with results

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Books' link in the header navigation
Submit the global search form in the header (e.g., type 'mystery' and press Enter)
Click a home page call-to-action such as 'Explore Fantasy' or 'Browse Science Fiction eBooks'
Click on a featured book cover or title
Click the 'Blog' link in the header or a 'View all posts' button in the featured blog section
Click a featured blog post title or 'Read more' link
Click the 'Events' link in the header or a 'View all events' button
Click an upcoming event title in the home page events list
Click the 'Library' link in the header navigation
Click the 'About' link in the header navigation
Click the 'Contact' link in the header or footer

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Home' link in the main header navigation and by clicking the site logo
Directly loaded when visiting the root URL or index.html

pages.name: Books & Search
pages.description: Main catalog and search page for browsing all books with filters, sorting, and list-building actions. Supports genre- and format-specific discovery, price and rating constraints, date and page-count ranges, and list, compare, and bundle operations used across the defined tasks.
primary_functions: Browse and search all books, including genre-based navigation and keyword search results (e.g., for 'mystery')
Filter books by genre (e.g., fantasy, science_fiction, young_adult), format (ebook, paperback), price range, rating, page count, and publication date range (e.g., 20182020)
Sort books by criteria such as publication date (newest first), price (low to high), and rating (highest first)
Add books directly from the listing to reading lists, wishlists, favorites, compare queue, and eBook bundle selections
Open individual book detail pages by clicking a book cover or title

incoming_params.description: Optional keyword search term (e.g., 'mystery'); typically set by the global search form on the Home page.
Optional genre filter (e.g., 'fantasy', 'science_fiction', 'young_adult'); 'all' shows all genres.
Optional format filter (e.g., 'ebook', 'paperback').
Optional work type filter (e.g., 'short_story', 'novella', 'novel') used for the 'Short Story / Novella' selection.
Minimum price in the active currency for price range filtering.
Maximum price in the active currency for price range filtering (e.g., 18 for Task 1, 20 for Task 2, 10 for Task 5).
Minimum average star rating (e.g., 4.0 or 4.5).
Maximum average star rating when needed; often left unset.
Minimum page count filter used for length-based browsing.
Maximum page count (e.g., 120 pages when looking for short works in Task 3).
Publication start date (ISO date string) used for date range filtering (e.g., '2018-01-01').
Publication end date (ISO date string) used for date range filtering (e.g., '2020-12-31').
Sort mode for results, mapped to backend sort options (e.g., 'publication_date_desc', 'price_asc', 'rating_desc').
Current results page for pagination.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a book cover image or title in the search results grid or list
Click the 'Compare' bar/button that appears after adding at least two books to the compare set
Click the 'View Bundle' or 'Bundle Summary' bar after adding at least one eBook to a bundle
Click a 'View Cart' or mini-cart link after adding a book or bundle from the listing
Click a 'View List' link from a toast/dialog after adding a book to a reading list or wishlist

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Books' link in the main header navigation
Accessed via the 'Books' link in the footer pointing to books.html?genre=all
Target page for the global search form submitted from the Home page and potentially other pages
Linked from home page featured genre CTAs and other contextual links that pre-configure filters via URL parameters

pages.name: Book Detail
pages.description: Detailed page for a single book with full information, reviews, and primary actions. Central place for adding the book to reading lists, wishlists, favorites, bundles, cart, and for writing and viewing reviews.
primary_functions: Display full book information including description, genre, formats, price, page count, publication date, rating, and review highlights
Allow users to add the book to a reading list (e.g., Main Reading List), a wishlist (e.g., Summer 2025), or favorites via buttons or heart icons
Enable adding the book to an eBook bundle or directly to the cart where applicable
Let users write, rate, and submit reviews (including selecting star rating and entering review text)
Show existing reviews and related or recommended books, with navigation back to the Books & Search page

incoming_params.description: ID of the book to display. Passed from featured book cards, search results, compare table, or library lists.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'View Cart' or 'Go to Cart' button after adding the book or a related bundle to the cart
Click a 'View List' link after adding the book to a specific reading list or wishlist
Click a 'Back to results' or 'More in this genre' link from the book detail page

access_methods.description: Accessed when a user follows a link containing the 'id' query parameter of a book
Linked from featured books on the Home page, from Books & Search, Compare, and Library pages

pages.name: Compare Books
pages.description: Comparison view for books selected from the listing, shown side-by-side in a table. Helps users evaluate attributes like page count, price, rating, and format before deciding which book to favorite or view in detail.
primary_functions: Display up to a small set of selected books side-by-side with key attributes such as title, price, rating, page count, and format
Help users visually compare metrics like page count to identify the longer or shorter book
Provide per-book buttons (e.g., View Details) that link to each book9s detail page for further actions like favoriting

incoming_params.description: Optional ID of a specific compare set to display; if omitted, the active compare set is loaded.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'View Details' button under a book in the comparison table
Click a 'Back to results' or 'Continue browsing' link from the comparison page

access_methods.description: Accessed by clicking the 'Compare' bar/button that appears on the Books & Search page after books are added to compare
Can optionally be accessed via a URL that includes a compareSetId parameter

pages.name: Bundle Summary
pages.description: Summary and management page for a custom eBook bundle before adding it to the cart. Shows selected sci-fi eBooks or other bundle items, allows quantity adjustments, and provides the final 'Add Bundle to Cart' action.
primary_functions: List all books currently included in the user9s eBook bundle, including title, price, and format
Allow users to adjust quantities (e.g., set each selected eBook quantity to 1) and remove books from the bundle
Show bundle pricing summary and provide an action to add the complete bundle to the cart

incoming_params.description: Optional bundle ID to explicitly open; if omitted, the active bundle in progress is loaded.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Add Bundle to Cart' button after reviewing the bundle contents
Click a 'Add more sci-fi eBooks' or 'Continue browsing' link from the bundle page

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'View Bundle' or 'Bundle Summary' bar that appears on the Books & Search page after adding books to a bundle
Can also be loaded directly with an optional bundleId parameter

pages.name: Cart
pages.description: Shopping cart page for reviewing and adjusting items before checkout. Shows both single books and bundles as line items, with controls to update quantities, remove items, and start checkout.
primary_functions: Display all items in the cart including bundles and individual books with quantities and prices
Allow users to update item quantities, remove items, and see updated totals
Provide a clear next-step action toward checkout or confirming the order (even if implemented elsewhere)

incoming_params.description: Optional source indicator (e.g., 'book_detail', 'bundle', 'books') used for UI messaging like 'Item added to your cart'.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Continue shopping' link or button from the cart page
Click an 'Edit bundle' link on a bundle-type cart item

access_methods.description: Accessed via a cart icon or 'Cart' utility link in the site header
Accessed after adding items from Book Detail, Bundle Summary, or Books pages via a 'View Cart' button

pages.name: Events
pages.description: Listing and calendar page for upcoming events and appearances. Provides calendar-based navigation, event type filters (online vs in-person), and date sorting so users can find specific events such as the earliest online event in March 2025.
primary_functions: Show upcoming events in a list or calendar, including date, time, type (online or in_person), and brief details
Allow filtering by month/year (e.g., switching to March 2025) and event type (e.g., Online Only)
Enable sorting events by date (earliest first or latest first)
Provide links from each listed event to its Event Detail page

incoming_params.description: Month number (1–12) that controls the calendar view and event listing (e.g., 3 for March).
Full year value (e.g., 2025) used with the month parameter to select which events to show.
Optional event type filter such as 'online', 'in_person', or 'hybrid'.
Sort mode for events (e.g., 'start_datetime_asc' for earliest-first or 'start_datetime_desc').
Current page in the paginated events list.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click an event title or 'View details' link in the event list or calendar view

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Events' link in the main header navigation
Accessed via the 'Events' link in the footer
Linked from the Home page 'Upcoming Events' section for month-specific or event-type-specific browsing

pages.name: Event Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a specific event with full information and registration form. Shows event metadata such as date, time, type, and access instructions, and hosts the registration workflow used in event-related tasks.
primary_functions: Present full event details including title, description, date, time, event type (online or in_person), and access instructions if online
Provide a registration or RSVP form capturing name, email, and optional notes/questions (e.g., 'Ask about sequel')
Confirm successful registration visually on the page after submission

incoming_params.description: ID of the event to display. Passed from the Events listing or from an upcoming event link on the Home page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Back to calendar' or 'View all events in this month' link

access_methods.description: Accessed by following a link that includes an 'eventId' query parameter
Linked from the Events page list/calendar and from the Home page upcoming events section

pages.name: Blog
pages.description: Listing page for blog posts and articles, including filters and sorting. Enables browsing by tags such as 'Writing Process' and sorting to find the latest or most discussed posts.
primary_functions: Display a list or grid of blog posts with titles, excerpts, dates, tags, and comment counts
Allow filtering posts by tag or category (e.g., selecting the writing_process tag)
Allow sorting posts by newest first or other criteria such as most commented
Enable navigation to individual article pages by clicking a post title

incoming_params.description: Optional tag slug filter (e.g., 'writing_process') to limit posts to a specific topic.
Sort mode for blog posts, mapped to backend options like 'published_at_desc' or 'comment_count_desc'.
Current page in the paginated blog post listing.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a post title, thumbnail, or 'Read more' link

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Blog' link in the main header navigation
Accessed via the 'Blog' link in the footer
Linked from the Home page featured blog posts section when navigating to view all posts with a specific tag

pages.name: Article Detail
pages.description: Full article page for a single blog post with comments and saving options. Displays the complete article content, associated comments, and actions to comment or save the article to a reading list.
primary_functions: Show the complete article content along with metadata such as date, tags, and author
Display existing comments and allow users to read through discussion
Provide a way to add new comments or replies if desired
Offer a 'Save to Reading List' action and a dialog to choose or create a reading list (e.g., Articles to Read) and confirm saving

incoming_params.description: ID of the article to display. Passed from the Blog listing, Home page featured posts, or the Library page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Back to blog' or 'More in this tag' link near the article header or footer
Click a 'View reading list' link after saving the article to a list such as 'Articles to Read'

access_methods.description: Accessed via a URL that includes the 'articleId' query parameter
Linked from the Blog page, Home page featured posts, and Library article reading lists

pages.name: Library
pages.description: Personal library hub for managing reading lists, wishlists, favorites, and saved articles. Provides an overview of all lists and detailed views of each list’s items, with controls to create, rename, delete, and move list items.
primary_functions: Display all user lists including reading lists (e.g., Main Reading List), wishlists (e.g., Summer 2025), favorites, and saved articles in organized sections or tabs
Allow creating new lists, renaming existing lists, and deleting lists that are no longer needed
Show items within each list and provide navigation to each item9s detail page (book or article)
Support removing items from lists or moving items between lists (e.g., from a wishlist to a main reading list)

incoming_params.description: Optional ID of a specific list to focus on (e.g., 'Main Reading List' or 'Summer 2025'). Used when users click 'View List' after saving an item.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a book title or cover from one of the reading lists, wishlists, or favorites
Click an article title from an article reading list such as 'Articles to Read'
Click a 'Find similar books' link from a list item

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Library' link in the main header navigation
Accessed via the 'Library' link in the footer
Linked from dialogs and toasts shown after adding books or articles to lists (e.g., 'View "Summer 2025" wishlist')

pages.name: Contact
pages.description: Contact form page for reaching the author with various topics including bulk orders. Hosts the topic dropdown and input fields required to send structured inquiries, such as bulk order requests for 25 paperbacks.
primary_functions: Provide a contact form with fields for topic/reason (including a Bulk Orders option), name, email, subject, and message body
Allow users to send specific inquiries such as a bulk order request for 25 paperbacks
Display confirmation that the message has been submitted and optionally show alternative contact methods

incoming_params.description: Optional topic preset value (e.g., 'bulk_orders', 'events') used to preselect the topic dropdown.
Optional subject line preset to help users contact the author about a specific FAQ item.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Return to Home' link after successfully submitting a contact message

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Contact' link in the main header navigation
Accessed via the 'Contact' link in the footer
Linked from FAQ answers or event pages when suggesting users get in touch

pages.name: About
pages.description: Information page about the author, their work, and their background. Uses dynamic content to present biography, writing background, achievements, and notable books with links to details.
primary_functions: Introduce the author with a biography, writing background, and key achievements
Highlight notable books and series with links to their detail pages
Provide context for the site9s purpose and invite users to explore books, blog, and events

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Browse all books' button from the About page
Click a notable book cover or title in the About page
Click a 'Read the blog' link to learn more about the writing process

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'About' link in the main header navigation
Accessed via the 'About' link in the footer

pages.name: FAQ
pages.description: Frequently Asked Questions page to help users quickly find answers. Covers questions about book formats, lists, bundles, events, and ordering, including guidance for bulk orders.
primary_functions: Answer common questions about book formats, reading lists, wishlists, bundles, and events
Address typical ordering and bulk order questions to complement the Contact page
Direct users to the appropriate pages (e.g., Contact, Books, Events) for more detailed actions

incoming_params.description: Optional FAQ category filter (e.g., 'bulk_orders', 'bundles', 'events') to focus on specific topics.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Contact us about bulk orders' link in a bulk order FAQ entry
Click a 'Browse books' link from FAQs related to formats or purchasing
Click a 'View upcoming events' link in an events-related FAQ answer

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'FAQ' link in the footer
Linked from Help or support mentions elsewhere on the site

pages.name: Policies
pages.description: Policy page covering privacy, data use, and terms for site usage. Includes review guidelines relevant to submitting book reviews and blog comments.
primary_functions: Explain how user data (such as reviews, reading lists, and registration details) is collected and used
Outline terms of use for the website, including content and review guidelines
Provide any legal notices or disclaimers related to purchases, events, or communications

incoming_params.description: Optional section anchor (e.g., 'privacy', 'terms', 'reviews') to scroll directly to a relevant policy section.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Contact the author' or 'Questions about these policies' link from the Policies page

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Policies' link in the footer
Linked from review and comment forms when referencing guidelines and terms

header_links.description: Homepage with featured books, posts, and events
Manage reading lists, wishlists, favorites, and saved articles
Contact the author or send inquiries

footer_links.description: Browse the full catalog of books
Privacy policy and terms of use
